_readme: Hand-labeled by reading raw model output directly (run_experiment_guide.md Step 2), before trusting extractor.py output. label = the letter a human reading raw text judges the model actually answered for THIS question, ignoring anything the model generates after that first coherent answer (invented questions, restated letters, degeneration loops). null means genuinely ambiguous or unparseable to a human, not just to the current extractor. note flags a raw-text shape worth keeping in mind for extractor edge cases; it is not a defect report. raw holds the exact raw_output string for each condition, parsed programmatically out of the source probe.log (see parse_probe.py at the time this file was built) so that test_extractor_probe.py can run the actual parsers against it rather than trusting the label in isolation.

12.subject: formal_logic
12.note: direct answers A then immediately invents a new question with zero scaffolding, no 'Answer:' marker at all - just the letter then straight into new question text. Oracle's degeneration here is a repeated phrase lifted from the question itself ('Marco moves from Italy to Spain...'), not token/punctuation garbage - a distinct degeneration flavor worth having in the test set.
